# UC-12.1.24 · GitLab Container Registry Garbage Collection and Blob Errors

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Fault &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch packages and artifacts so supply-chain risk, storage, and policy checks stay under control.*

---

## Description

GC and blob errors risk storage exhaustion and failed image pulls for production clusters.

## Value

Catches registry maintenance failures that would break image pulls during deploys.

## Implementation

Ship registry logs to Splunk; truncate `message_snippet` at ingest. Page on sustained error bursts correlated with disk alerts.

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:registry" OR (sourcetype="gitlab:application" component="registry")
| search "blob" AND ("delete" OR "gc" OR "garbage" OR "manifest unknown" OR "unknown blob")
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_seen by host, message_snippet
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (error categories), Timeline of registry errors.

## Known False Positives

Activity shifts during holidays, release freezes, or vendor maintenance; confirm against your change calendar.

## References

- [GitLab Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6848)
- [GitLab CI/CD logs to Splunk (Lantern)](https://lantern.splunk.com/Data_Descriptors/GitLab/Getting_Gitlab_CICD_data_out_of_a_Gitlab_Pipeline_into_Splunk)
